title: Parents knew where their children were; responsibilities of kids at home
description: Parents knew where their children were when Mr. Settle was young. Responsibilities of kids at home.

title: Going to a one room schoolhouse; eight kids and one teacher at Aspendale; how he got to school in different seasons
description: One room schoolhouse. Eighty kids and one teacher at Aspendale. Carrying the booksack. Winter play at school. Getting to school- loading up a wagon with kids. Plowing snow; riding horses in winter. German socks.

title: Favorite subjects in school; most children being moved back a grade when they moved to town; taking the eighth grade exam
description: Favorite subjects, spelling bees. Limited opportunity for teacher to work with slower children. Most children were put back a grade when they moved to town. Taking the eighth grade exam, after working with the teacher on arithmetic.

title: The importance of a good education
description: Importance of getting good education to parents; while living in the country they couldn't go to high school.

title: Why his parents moved near town; his education ended with World War I
description: His education ended with the war. Parents moved near town partly so kids could get an education.

title: Decisions were made by parents in partnership; parents' religious beliefs; getting lost at a camp meeting
description: Decisions were made by parents in partnership. Parents' religious beliefs; devout Baptists, keeping Sabbath, teaching the children. Getting lost at a camp meeting.

title: Mother was the disciplinarian and bought what they needed; parents didn't disagree; father enjoyed syrup
description: Mother mostly disciplined the children and bought what was needed for them. Parents didn't disagree. The very few staples that they bought in the store. Father's enjoyment of syrup.

title: Their home location and its conveniences for his mother
description: Home location. Conveniences of the new home for his mother.

title: Bringing country and town together; wishes he was still living in the country; school consolidation; differences between town and country people
description: Bringing country and town together. He wishes he was still living in the country. School consolidation. Differences between town and country people in town.

title: Discussion about country people in town; he wanted enough clothes to look like a town kid
description: Country people in town. Country people who wore overalls until they wore out. "Hayseeds" and "pumpkin rollers." He wanted to have enough clothes to look like a town kid.
